servers.label: Home Assistant

auth.hint: Create a Long-Lived Access Token under your HA profile → Security.

servers.notes: Your local Home Assistant instance. Discovered tools are screened by the egress + tool-poisoning screen before the AI can use them.

servers.label: Slack

auth.hint: Bearer token your self-hosted Slack MCP server expects (e.g. a bot token).

servers.notes: Point this at your own Slack MCP server. Outbound arguments are screened so tokens and PII don't leave the device.

servers.label: Notion

auth.hint: Notion uses OAuth. Sign-in automation is coming; for now paste an access token in the editor.

servers.notes: Notion's hosted MCP endpoint. OAuth device-code sign-in is deferred (Plan V follow-up).

servers.label: GitHub

auth.hint: GitHub uses OAuth. Sign-in automation is coming; for now paste a personal access token in the editor.

servers.notes: GitHub's hosted MCP endpoint. OAuth device-code sign-in is deferred (Plan V follow-up).

servers.label: Linear

auth.hint: Linear uses OAuth over SSE. Both the OAuth flow and the live SSE stream are deferred (Plan V follow-up).

servers.notes: Linear's hosted MCP endpoint. Listed for completeness; live connection lands when SSE + OAuth ship.
